title: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
stakeholder_needs:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders: (1) End users/homeowners want convenient quick-clean, low fatigue (<=1.5 kg), adequate suction, simple maintenance, low noise, and clear battery/runtime expectations (>=20 min) with reliable performance over battery life. (2) Safety/regulatory stakeholders require EU compliance: CE marking under applicable directives/regulations (e.g., Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, Battery Regulation, WEEE), plus harmonized standards (e.g., EN/IEC 60335 series for household appliances, EN 62233 EMF, EN 55014 EMC). (3) Manufacturing/QA need testable, measurable requirements (runtime test conditions, weight definition incl. battery/accessories, dust capacity, durability, drop/ingress, charging safety). (4) Service/support and sustainability stakeholders care about battery replacement/repair, recyclability, documentation, and warranty. Initial requirements should be measurable and verifiable, but some critical parameters are missing (runtime conditions, suction performance targets, charging method, and exact regulatory scope).
clarification_questions: Define the runtime test conditions for the 20-minute requirement (power mode, surface type, with/without brush head, new vs. aged battery, ambient temperature).
What suction/performance target is required (e.g., air watts/kPa/CFM, debris pickup on hard floor vs. carpet, dust bin capacity)?
What charging and battery expectations apply (USB-C vs. dock, charge time, removable vs. sealed battery, battery chemistry, and any constraints on noise level)?

table.title: DFMEA — Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
rows: Battery pack SHALL prevent thermal runaway
Motor SHALL maintain suction under nominal debris load
Loss of suction; motor overheating; shutdown
Debris bypass inlet grille; filter saturated
Inlet grille mesh 4 mm max; filter capacity analysis
Stall detection in firmware; EOL suction test
Filter SHALL trap >= 99% of particles >= 0.3 µm
Dust emission; user exposure; EU standard non-compliance
Keyed filter housing; seal torque specification
Charging system SHALL restore full charge within 4 h
Battery damage; safety event; reduced battery life
Certified charger IC; charge profile validation
Housing SHALL withstand 1 m drop onto hard floor without cracking
Loss of structural integrity; user injury; ingress of debris
FEA drop simulation; material selection review
1 m drop test on all faces (6 faces)

messages.content:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.

Stakeholders: (1) End users/homeowners want convenient quick-clean, low fatigue (<=1.5 kg), adequate suction, simple maintenance, low noise, and clear battery/runtime expectations (>=20 min) with reliable performance over battery life. (2) Safety/regulatory stakeholders require EU compliance: CE marking under applicable directives/regulations (e.g., Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, Battery Regulation, WEEE), plus harmonized standards (e.g., EN/IEC 60335 series for household appliances, EN 62233 EMF, EN 55014 EMC). (3) Manufacturing/QA need testable, measurable requirements (runtime test conditions, weight definition incl. battery/accessories, dust capacity, durability, drop/ingress, charging safety). (4) Service/support and sustainability stakeholders care about battery replacement/repair, recyclability, documentation, and warranty. Initial requirements should be measurable and verifiable, but some critical parameters are missing (runtime conditions, suction performance targets, charging method, and exact regulatory scope).
